turns.content: What brings you in today?
Well, doc, it's my knees. They ache something fierce, especially when I first wake up in the morning. Getting out of bed is a real struggle.
When did this start?
It's been coming on gradual-like for a couple years now. But this past year, the morning stiffness is getting worse. I just figure it's old age, but it's harder to ignore these days.
Can you describe the pain or discomfort? Where exactly do you feel it?
It's mostly right on top of the kneecaps, both of 'em. Feels like a deep, dull ache, not a sharp stab. Sometimes it grinds a little when I move 'em, like gravel rolling around in there.
Have you had any fever or chills?
No, nothing like that. Just the usual ache. No fever or chills - I'd know if I was really sick.
What medications are you currently taking?
I take a little yellow pill for my blood pressure, and one for my sugar - the doctor said it's for my diabetes. That's all. I don't like taking too many pills.
Do you have any chronic medical conditions?
Well, like I said, I got high blood pressure and that sugar problem - diabetes. Been managing them for years. Nothing else real serious.
Are you allergic to any medications?
Not that I know of. I've never had a bad reaction to any pill I've taken.
Have you tried anything to relieve the symptoms?
I just take it easy for a bit. I put a hot water bottle on 'em when they get real bad, and that helps some. I also try to stretch a little before I get up, but it's hard.
Does anything make it better or worse?

Well, moving around some after I've been sitting a while helps loosen 'em up, but if I overdo it, like walking too far or standing too long, they start to ache worse. Cold weather makes 'em stiffer too, I've noticed.
How has this been affecting your daily life?

It's slowing me down, that's for sure. Used to take care of my little garden out back, but now I can't kneel or squat to pull weeds. Even carrying groceries from the car is a chore. I just do what I gotta do and rest the rest
Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
No, I never picked up smoking. I do have a glass of wine now and then, maybe once or twice a week with dinner. Nothing heavy.
Has anyone in your family had similar problems?

My mother, God rest her soul, had trouble with her knees too. She used to say it was the curse of getting older. Never had it looked at proper, far as I know. Nobody else in the family complained much about it.
Have you experienced anything like this before?
Like I said, it's been building for a couple years now. But nothing this bad before. Never had an injury or anything - just seems to be wear and tear from all them years on my feet at the factory.
On a scale of 1 to 10, how would you rate what you're going through right now — physically, and also emotionally?

Physically, I'd say it's a solid 7. It's there all the time, nagging at me. Emotionally... maybe a 4. I'm not scared or anything, just tired of it. Figure it's part of getting older, so I don't let it get me down too much.
Is there anything else you think I should know?

No, I think that's about it, doc. Just my knees giving me trouble. You know what it's like getting older - things start to wear out. Just hoping you can tell me if there's anything to do besides just putting up with it.
And how have you been coping with all of this?
I just take it day by day, doc. No use complaining about what I can't change. I rest when I need to and keep moving when I can. Some days are better than others, but I manage. That's just how it is.
And how have you been coping with all of this?

Oh, I just keep on keepin' on. I get up a little slower now, hold onto the bed frame, and once I'm up and moving, it's manageable. I don't let it stop me from doing what has to be done. No point in fussing over it.
And how have you been coping with all of this?
I told you, I just get on with it. No sense in making a big fuss. I rest when I can and keep going when I have to. That's all there is to it.
And how have you been coping with all of this?
I already told you - I just manage. I get up slow, I rest when I need to, and I don't let it get me down. Not much else I can do about it, is there?
And how have you been coping with all of this?
I'm sorry, doc, but I already answered that a few times now. I just get through it the best I can - rest, move slow, don't complain. Is there something else you need to know about my knees?
